target.name: Foundation Robotics
target.founder: Sankaet Pathak, Arjun Sethi, Mike LeBlanc

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $3.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Foundation Robotics, a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Sankaet Pathak, Arjun Sethi, and Mike LeBlanc, is raising at a reported $3.0B post-money valuation. The valuation assessment is grounded in a peer set of 9 comparable companies—1 public and 8 private—spanning robotics and manufacturing, including later-stage reference points such as 1X Technologies ($10.0B Series B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B Series B), and Dexterity ($1.6B Series B).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $3.0B post-money valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent stage advancement, implies a forward valuation of $6.0B. Peer-median analysis across the 6-company robotics and manufacturing comparable set (median $1.1B; range $300M–$10.0B) yields a $1.1B indication. These divergent signals reflect the wide dispersion in peer valuations and the inherent stage-maturity gap between Foundation Robotics' Series A position and later-stage peers.

Triangulation of both methods produces a median implied valuation of $3.5B (range: $914M–$6.9B), which brackets the reported $3.0B raise valuation. Key uncertainties include the composition and maturity of the peer set—particularly reliance on later-stage comparables—and whether Foundation Robotics commands a stage premium relative to the broader peer median. The current reported valuation sits near the lower bound of the triangulated range.
